jokes.text: And I am also a registered sex offender.
jokes.setup: Tobias introducing himself as a new therapist to rehab patients
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions being a registered sex offender as part of his introduction
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-driven cringe comedy. The casual delivery of something so inappropriate establishes Tobias's complete lack of self-awareness while creating maximum discomfort.

jokes.text: Now, let's have a wrap-about.
jokes.setup: Tobias trying to lead a therapy session
jokes.punchline: Says 'wrap-about' instead of 'rap-about' or group discussion
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias malapropism that reveals his pretentious vocabulary getting in the way of basic competence.

jokes.text: Daddy needs to get his rocks...
jokes.setup: Flashback to Tobias's arrest, him looking for a little girl
jokes.punchline: The innocent explanation (geology) is cut off, leaving the worst possible implication
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that plays with audience expectations. The cut-off creates maximum uncomfortable comedy while maintaining Tobias's innocence.

jokes.text: Oh, is this going to be SAG or AFTRA?
jokes.setup: Being arrested by police
jokes.punchline: Instead of asking for a lawyer, asks about union jurisdiction
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Tobias's misplaced priorities - even during arrest, he's thinking about his acting career. Shows his complete disconnect from reality.

jokes.text: Anus tart. - Yep, that's me.
jokes.setup: Someone recognizing Tobias from his license plate
jokes.punchline: Tobias proudly confirms his identification with the unfortunate double entendre
jokes.explanation: Classic callback to one of the show's most famous running gags. Tobias's obliviousness to the innuendo makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: a theralyst.
jokes.setup: Tobias explaining he's both an analyst and therapist
jokes.punchline: Creates the portmanteau 'theralyst' instead of the obvious inappropriate one
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of expectations. Audience expects 'therapist' but gets a new, innocent portmanteau. Shows Tobias learned something in prison.

jokes.text: Ah, well within my rights.
jokes.setup: Asked about a young woman's age (23)
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately comments on being 'within his rights'
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy that plays on Tobias's complete lack of appropriate boundaries. The casual delivery makes it more disturbing and funny.

jokes.text: Oh, and Simon says you're out.
jokes.setup: Patient clarifies his name is Mark Cherry, first and last
jokes.punchline: Tobias treats it like a game of Simon Says
jokes.explanation: Absurd logic that shows Tobias's childlike approach to serious situations. The randomness works because it's character-consistent.

jokes.text: Sometimes I have to tell people 'cause they can't figure it out from when I say the things that I...
jokes.setup: Tobias claiming to have wit
jokes.punchline: Rambling explanation that proves he's not witty at all
jokes.explanation: Self-defeating statement where Tobias's explanation undermines his claim. The trailing off makes it funnier.

jokes.text: ♪ Go away, Getaway, get away, Getaway. ♪
jokes.setup: Mark Cherry talking about getting away from drugs
jokes.punchline: Breaks into an inappropriate musical number about getting away
jokes.explanation: Character-specific cringe as Mark Cherry can't help but turn everything into a song. Sets up his musical theater background.

jokes.text: I'm not Andy (bleep)ing Richter. I'm Emmett.
jokes.setup: Patient who looks identical to Andy Richter
jokes.punchline: Angrily denies being Andy Richter while clearly being his lookalike
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about Andy Richter (who works with the narrator) appearing in the show. The angry denial while being obviously identical is absurd.

jokes.text: Simon says you're out.
jokes.setup: Emmett gives his last name (Richter)
jokes.punchline: Tobias again treats normal conversation like Simon Says
jokes.explanation: Running gag that shows Tobias's complete failure to learn from previous interactions. Repetition works because it's character-driven.

jokes.text: Could you give me one second to organically get...? And I'm also a registered sex offender.
jokes.setup: Tobias trying to naturally work something into conversation
jokes.punchline: The 'organic' reveal is that he's a sex offender
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that shows Tobias's complete misunderstanding of what should be revealed 'organically.' The pause makes it funnier.

jokes.text: But I love her. Surely there must be some way we can be together.
jokes.setup: Being told he can't be DeBrie's therapist
jokes.punchline: Immediately declares his love inappropriately in front of everyone
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Tobias's complete lack of professional boundaries and social awareness.

jokes.text: ♪ Hot cops, we'll clean up the town... ♪ I ain't no squealer.
jokes.setup: Tobias showing off his acting reel
jokes.punchline: Mix of singing Hot Cops theme and dramatic one-liner
jokes.explanation: Callback to iconic moments from the show's history. The contrast between the cheery song and tough guy line is absurd.

jokes.text: I got here soon as I could. I'm Dr. House, and it's called GVH. The graft is rejecting the host.
jokes.setup: More of Tobias's acting reel
jokes.punchline: Terrible impression of Dr. House with medical nonsense
jokes.explanation: Shows Tobias's delusional confidence in his acting ability. The medical terminology is meaningless but delivered with authority.

jokes.text: But like a dog's wagging tail, Argyle's tapping feet betrayed his true feelings.
jokes.setup: Argyle claiming reluctance about the musical
jokes.punchline: Visual reveal that his feet are tapping, showing he's excited
jokes.explanation: Classic Arrested Development visual storytelling. The dog comparison makes the human behavior more absurd.

jokes.text: She was in the movie The Fantastic Four. Oh, my God. I was in The Fantasticks.
jokes.setup: Tobias mentioning DeBrie was in Fantastic Four
jokes.punchline: Argyle's excited response about being in The Fantasticks (completely different)
jokes.explanation: Wordplay confusion between similar titles. Argyle's enthusiasm for the tenuous connection is character-driven comedy.

jokes.text: ♪Mr. F.♪
jokes.setup: Reference to Mr. Fantastic
jokes.punchline: Musical sting callback to 'Mr. F' from earlier seasons
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that rewards longtime viewers. The musical sting format is iconic to the show.

jokes.text: It's Tobias' Arrested Development.
jokes.setup: Standard Arrested Development title card setup
jokes.punchline: Makes it about Tobias specifically instead of the family
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke that plays with the show's own format. Works both as character joke and self-referential humor.

jokes.text: Well, that's a good luck sign on Broadway. On the East Coast it's 'break a leg,' on the West Coast it's 'lose a tooth.'
jokes.setup: DeBrie losing a tooth
jokes.punchline: Tobias creates fake theater superstition about losing teeth being good luck
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias fake expertise. The geographic distinction makes the made-up fact more believable and absurd.

jokes.text: On the East Coast it's 'break a leg,' on the West Coast it's 'lose a tooth.'
jokes.setup: Explaining the 'good luck' tradition
jokes.punchline: Making up a completely false West Coast tradition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias logic - confidently stating completely made-up facts.

jokes.text: Beard! Beard, I'm only here to pick up my car keys.
jokes.setup: Tobias arriving at Sudden Valley
jokes.punchline: Calls out 'Beard!' as if it's someone's name
jokes.explanation: Absurd moment where Tobias treats 'Beard' like a person's name. Shows his disconnect from normal social interaction.

jokes.text: the one she owns, not the one she famously went to 12 times.
jokes.setup: Mentioning Lucille 2's rehab center
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that she famously went to rehab 12 times
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about addiction delivered casually. The number '12' is excessive enough to be funny.

jokes.text: True, I can't ethically ravage her body. I have to leave that to time
jokes.setup: Tobias talking about his relationship with DeBrie
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate way of describing ethical boundaries and aging
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny way for Tobias to describe professional ethics. The 'leave that to time' makes it worse/funnier.

jokes.text: there are no schools or libraries or Internet or playgrounds within 20 miles of here.
jokes.setup: Gob complaining about selling houses in Sudden Valley
jokes.punchline: Lists exactly what would appeal to a sex offender (isolation from these places)
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for Tobias's response. Gob unknowingly describes exactly what Tobias needs as a sex offender.

jokes.text: it's funny, I've been looking for a place to live, and that fills virtually every parole requirement I have.
jokes.setup: Gob's complaint about the isolation of Sudden Valley
jokes.punchline: Tobias reveals this is exactly what he needs for parole
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic reversal where Gob's problem becomes Tobias's solution. Excellent structure and timing.

jokes.text: And this will make my stay shorter, right? I'll get out of here a week sooner?
jokes.setup: Mark Cherry being told about the musical project
jokes.punchline: Immediately focused on how it benefits him personally, not the art
jokes.explanation: Character-driven selfishness that undercuts the supposed artistic endeavor. Shows his real priorities.

jokes.text: Am I crazy, or is this really good?
jokes.setup: Terrible musical performance we just witnessed
jokes.punchline: Tobias thinks it's genuinely good
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony where Tobias's assessment is completely wrong. The audience knows it's terrible.

jokes.text: See, you keep writing this infantile, ridiculous melody over and over and over again.
jokes.setup: Tobias critiquing Mark Cherry's music
jokes.punchline: Harsh criticism delivered in his pompous therapeutic voice
jokes.explanation: Tobias's tendency to be brutally honest in the worst way possible. The repetition emphasis makes it harsher.

jokes.text: As your therapist, I'm happy that you're expressing yourself. But as a director, I don't have to like it. And I don't. But, also, good for you. Therapist now speaking. But, also, no.
jokes.setup: Tobias trying to balance his roles as therapist and director
jokes.punchline: Confusing back-and-forth that makes no sense and ends negatively
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Tobias's inability to maintain professional boundaries. The contradictory statements escalate perfectly.

jokes.text: Am I crazy, or is this good? It's really good.
jokes.setup: Another terrible performance
jokes.punchline: Tobias asks the same question and gets agreement
jokes.explanation: Running gag that shows both characters are delusional about their artistic abilities.

jokes.text: Am I crazy, or is this good? - It's really good.
jokes.setup: Tobias asking about their amateur performance
jokes.punchline: Both being completely delusional about the quality
jokes.explanation: Repetition of earlier delusion, showing both characters' lack of perspective.

jokes.text: Now what if, say, I could raise between $50 and $100?
jokes.setup: Being told the production would cost $700,000
jokes.punchline: Offers to contribute a laughably small amount
jokes.explanation: Perfect scale comedy showing how out of touch Tobias is with money and production costs.

jokes.text: Hey! I can whistle. Ooh, we have to use that in the show.
jokes.setup: Serious conversation about production costs and rights
jokes.punchline: Mark Cherry interrupts with excitement about being able to whistle
jokes.explanation: Character-driven interruption that shows Mark Cherry's misplaced priorities and childlike enthusiasm.

jokes.text: Three of us? Houston, we're having a baby.
jokes.setup: Watching the original Fantastic Four movie
jokes.punchline: Terrible line delivery in what's clearly a bad movie
jokes.explanation: Shows the quality of the original movie they're adapting. The bad line delivery is the joke.

jokes.text: Say hi to Ron Howard for me.
jokes.setup: Michael thinking Ron Howard is Rebel's boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically tells her to say hi, not knowing Ron is her father
jokes.explanation: Great dramatic irony where Michael's jealous sarcasm is actually perfectly appropriate advice.

jokes.text: Like the $18 baked potato you ordered that you didn't touch.
jokes.setup: Michael arguing with his date about comfort
jokes.punchline: Specific callback to expensive, untouched food
jokes.explanation: Good observational detail that rings true for expensive restaurant awkwardness.

jokes.text: family first, unless there's a work thing, and then work first.
jokes.setup: Michael saying 'family first' as usual
jokes.punchline: George Michael adds the cynical caveat about work
jokes.explanation: Perfect character development showing George Michael has learned his father's real priorities. The structure is excellent.

jokes.text: I'm a tall drink of water, and he's the chaser. Should be flipped, right?
jokes.setup: Introducing herself and her much taller brother
jokes.punchline: Self-aware joke about their height difference making the metaphor wrong
jokes.explanation: Well-constructed joke that shows self-awareness. The setup and punchline work perfectly together.

jokes.text: you owe my sister $700,000, and I'm going to chase you down until you pay up.
jokes.setup: The chaser joke from his sister
jokes.punchline: Reveals the threatening meaning - he'll chase Michael for money
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that gives the previous joke a threatening edge. The structure works well.

jokes.text: Taps. And I can tap dance all over your face, if you know what I mean. Even if my heart won't snap back into place.
jokes.setup: Michael asking about what's on the bottom of Argyle's shoes
jokes.punchline: Reveals tap shoes and makes dancing threat with song lyric
jokes.explanation: Character-driven threat that incorporates musical theater background. The song lyric addition makes it absurd.

jokes.text: ♪Mr. F.♪ Argyle was wrong on that count. It was Buster who wore the gorilla suit.
jokes.setup: Argyle threatening Michael about hiding in a gorilla suit
jokes.punchline: Narrator corrects him - it's Buster in the gorilla suit
jokes.explanation: Good callback that connects to ongoing subplot. The narrator's correction is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Hello, anus tart. And she never even saw the license plate.
jokes.setup: Lucille Austero greeting Tobias
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals she doesn't even know about his license plate
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with added irony. The fact that she doesn't know about the plate makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Me doth think he hath a lady. As doth I dooth.
jokes.setup: Tobias trying to speak in Shakespearean English
jokes.punchline: Gets the grammar completely wrong with 'dooth'
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias pseudo-intellectualism failing. The 'dooth' is perfectly wrong and character-consistent.

jokes.text: we are (bleep). You're (bleep). (bleep). I mean, unless some sort of miracle coincidence happens. But, no, the best thing to do is just walk away,
jokes.setup: Tobias trying to give Michael relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Repeated bleeped swearing escalating to complete hopelessness
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing Tobias's pessimism overwhelming his therapeutic training. The escalation works.

jokes.text: Reverse psychology. It didn't work.
jokes.setup: Michael questioning why Tobias changed his advice
jokes.punchline: Admits he was trying reverse psychology and it failed
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan admission of therapeutic failure. The economy and honesty make it funny.

jokes.text: I will act as your assistant and maybe I'll do my famous gay character. - I don't think you've seen that character. - I think I have.
jokes.setup: Tobias offering to play a gay character
jokes.punchline: Michael knowing Tobias always seems gay
jokes.explanation: Great callback to long-running character trait with perfect timing.

jokes.text: I think I have. Mm... I don't think so.
jokes.setup: Tobias claiming Michael hasn't seen his gay character
jokes.punchline: Michael's confident assertion followed by Tobias's denial
jokes.explanation: Running gag about Tobias's lack of self-awareness. The back-and-forth is character-perfect.

jokes.text: This is a hat-on haircut, so you just cut what they can see. You got it, Floyd? I call all my barbers Floyd.
jokes.setup: Ron Howard getting a haircut while wearing his signature baseball cap
jokes.punchline: Explains the hat-on haircut concept and calls all barbers Floyd
jokes.explanation: Great character detail that explains Ron Howard's perpetual hat-wearing. The Floyd detail adds extra absurdity.

jokes.text: And as it is such, so also as such is it unto you
jokes.setup: Tobias describing his acting roles
jokes.punchline: Mentions playing pseudo-biblical nonsense dialogue roles
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Tobias's pretentious roles that sound important but mean nothing. The archaic language is spot-on.

jokes.text: Did you not know that I'm Rebel's dad? I knew that part.
jokes.setup: Michael's confused conversation about Rebel
jokes.punchline: Ron reveals he's her father; Michael claims he knew
jokes.explanation: Great revelation moment with Michael's transparent lie. The structure builds tension perfectly.

jokes.text: Did you not know that I'm Rebel's dad?
jokes.setup: Michael's romantic confession about Rebel
jokes.punchline: The revelation that Ron is her father, not boyfriend
jokes.explanation: Major revelation that recontextualizes the entire episode's misunderstanding.

jokes.text: Her lover? Well, this is hardly an episode of Happy Days.
jokes.setup: Michael suggesting Ron might be Rebel's lover
jokes.punchline: Ron references Happy Days (which he was in) about inappropriate relationships
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke referencing Ron Howard's own career and the inappropriate relationships on Happy Days. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: I was arrested as a sex offender, but at least I knew it was my daughter I was arrested for coming on to.
jokes.setup: Trying to make Michael feel better about his situation
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was arrested for coming on to his own daughter
jokes.explanation: Extremely dark comedy that somehow makes Tobias's situation worse than Michael's. The casual delivery is perfect.

jokes.text: which, with a wave of your little pink hand, you could make happen
jokes.setup: Tobias asking Ron for the rights
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific description of Ron's hand
jokes.explanation: Typical Tobias weirdly specific physical observation that adds to the awkwardness.

jokes.text: I also don't want to.
jokes.setup: Ron saying he can't help with the Fantastic Four rights
jokes.punchline: Adds that he also doesn't want to help
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that goes from professional 'can't' to personal 'don't want to.' Brutally efficient.

jokes.text: You are ruining my life, Ron Howard!
jokes.setup: Ron Howard rejecting Tobias's request
jokes.punchline: Tobias dramatically blames Ron Howard for ruining his life
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias overreaction with dramatic flair. The full name usage makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Tobias Onyango Funke.
jokes.setup: Tobias signing documents
jokes.punchline: Reveals his middle name is Onyango
jokes.explanation: Random character detail that adds to Tobias's pretentious background. The African middle name is unexpected.

jokes.text: He's back. I got the rights. We're going to make a musical. Or just lie.
jokes.setup: Tobias announcing he got the rights
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals he's just lying
jokes.explanation: Perfect narrator misdirection. The economy of 'Or just lie' is brilliant and character-perfect for Tobias.

jokes.text: Maybe they should retitle this Fantastic Three and Lousy One.
jokes.setup: Critiquing DeBrie's performance
jokes.punchline: Suggests renaming the show to insult one performer
jokes.explanation: Cutting insult delivered in Lucille's characteristic cruel style. The wordplay on the title is clever.

jokes.text: In case you didn't know, the stage adds seven pounds. - Two words: diet pills.
jokes.setup: Lucille commenting on weight and stage appearance
jokes.punchline: Immediate suggestion of drugs to addicts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille moment - completely inappropriate suggestion to recovering addicts.

jokes.text: you're sure the Hispanic community is okay with all of this?
jokes.setup: About to perform on Cinco de Cuatro
jokes.punchline: Sudden awareness of potential cultural insensitivity
jokes.explanation: Last-minute cultural sensitivity concern that comes too late. The timing makes it cringe-funny.

jokes.text: The audience will be completely drunk, and if we're not perfect, they'll take it out on us, but... that's why you're going to be perfect.
jokes.setup: Tobias trying to calm DeBrie's nerves
jokes.punchline: Accidentally makes it worse by mentioning violence, then demands perfection
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of Tobias making situations worse while trying to help. The escalation from comfort to pressure is character-perfect.

jokes.text: I'm a registered sex offender. All right. I'm a registered... I'm a registered sex offender.
jokes.setup: Seeing children at the performance venue
jokes.punchline: Repeatedly announces he's a sex offender to clear the area
jokes.explanation: Callback that shows Tobias using his status practically for once. The repetition shows his desperation.

jokes.text: You're a monster, you don't have to be good. What? It wasn't a great choice of words.
jokes.setup: Tobias giving Buster direction for playing the Thing
jokes.punchline: Realizes he just called Buster a monster, immediately regrets it
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias foot-in-mouth moment with immediate self-awareness. The quick correction makes it funnier.

jokes.text: How could you do this to me? Or did I do this to you? You to me.
jokes.setup: Finding DeBrie has relapsed
jokes.punchline: Tobias's confused blame assignment, corrected by DeBrie
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Tobias's narcissistic confusion about responsibility. DeBrie's correction is brutal.

jokes.text: I just blue myself for the first time in five years.
jokes.setup: Tobias painting himself blue to play Sue Storm
jokes.punchline: References his blue man obsession with typical double entendre
jokes.explanation: Iconic callback to one of the show's most famous lines. The time reference adds poignancy and humor.

jokes.text: Nice ad-lib. Not an ad-lib... I can't swim.
jokes.setup: Someone praising what they think is improvisation
jokes.punchline: Buster reveals it's genuine panic about not being able to swim
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that reveals genuine character trait. The contrast between performance and reality is excellent.
